overview: Topological sort yields two phases: the leave-api has no dependencies and must be built first, while leave-web and notification-dispatcher both depend on it and can be built in parallel in phase 2.

phases.name: Foundation API
phases.description: Build the core backend service that all other components depend on.

components.name: leave-api
responsibilities: Authenticate users with JWT and enforce role-based access control
Manage leave request lifecycle (create, cancel, approve, reject) with balance, notice, blackout, and overlap validation
Maintain leave types, balances, blackouts, and HR balance adjustments with audit notes
Serve calendar, manager queue, reports (JSON/CSV/PDF), and immutable audit trails
Queue email notifications and expose pending/mark-sent endpoints for the dispatcher
components.architectureContext: Central backend REST API consumed by the leave-web SPA (user traffic) and polled by notification-dispatcher for pending emails. Owns all domain state in in-memory stores.
keyConsiderations: Use sync.RWMutex around in-memory maps to safely handle 500 concurrent users
Reserve balance on submission and release on rejection/cancellation to prevent race conditions
Enforce 5MB attachment cap and PDF/JPG/PNG mime validation for base64 uploads
Implement sliding 30-min JWT expiry and CORS for SPA access
Generate 3-day reminder notifications idempotently to avoid duplicates
components.apiContract:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Leave API
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      responses:
        '200': { description: OK }
  /auth/login:
    post:
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                email: { type: string }
                password: { type: string }
      responses:
        '200': { description: JWT token }
  /auth/logout:
    post:
      responses: { '204': { description: OK } }
  /auth/me:
    get:
      responses: { '200': { description: current user } }
  /users:
    get:
      responses: { '200': { description: list users } }
  /users/{id}/balances:
    get:
      parameters: [{ name: id, in: path, required: true, schema: { type: string } }]
      responses: { '200': { description: balances } }
  /users/{id}/balances/adjust:
    post:
      parameters: [{ name: id, in: path, required: true, schema: { type: string } }]
      responses: { '200': { description: adjusted } }
  /leave-types:
    get:
      responses: { '200': { description: list } }
    post:
      responses: { '201': { description: created } }
  /leave-types/{id}:
    put:
      parameters: [{ name: id, in: path, required: true, schema: { type: string } }]
      responses: { '200': { description: updated } }
  /leave-requests:
    get:
      responses: { '200': { description: list } }
    post:
      responses: { '201': { description: created } }
  /leave-requests/{id}:
    get:
      parameters: [{ name: id, in: path, required: true, schema: { type: string } }]
      responses: { '200': { description: detail } }
  /leave-requests/{id}/cancel:
    post:
      parameters: [{ name: id, in: path, required: true, schema: { type: string } }]
      responses: { '200': { description: cancelled } }
  /leave-requests/{id}/approve:
    post:
      parameters: [{ name: id, in: path, required: true, schema: { type: string } }]
      responses: { '200': { description: approved } }
  /leave-requests/{id}/reject:
    post:
      parameters: [{ name: id, in: path, required: true, schema: { type: string } }]
      responses: { '200': { description: rejected } }
  /leave-requests/{id}/attachments:
    post:
      parameters: [{ name: id, in: path, required: true, schema: { type: string } }]
      responses: { '201': { description: uploaded } }
  /calendar:
    get:
      responses: { '200': { description: calendar events } }
  /manager/queue:
    get:
      responses: { '200': { description: pending requests } }
  /manager/team-calendar:
    get:
      responses: { '200': { description: team events } }
  /policies/blackouts:
    get: { responses: { '200': { description: list } } }
    post: { responses: { '201': { description: created } } }
  /reports/utilization:
    get:
      responses: { '200': { description: report (json/csv/pdf via ?format=) } }
  /audit/{employeeId}:
    get:
      parameters: [{ name: employeeId, in: path, required: true, schema: { type: string } }]
      responses: { '200': { description: audit trail } }
  /notifications/pending:
    get:
      responses: { '200': { description: pending email notifications } }
  /notifications/{id}/mark-sent:
    post:
      parameters: [{ name: id, in: path, required: true, schema: { type: string } }]
      responses: { '204': { description: OK } }
  /notifications/inapp:
    get:
      responses: { '200': { description: in-app notifications } }

phases.name: Frontend and Notification Worker
phases.description: Build the user-facing SPA and the scheduled dispatcher in parallel, both consuming the leave-api.

components.name: leave-web
responsibilities: Provide login and role-aware dashboards for Employee, Manager, and HR Admin
Enable leave request creation, cancellation, and file attachment workflows
Render team/company calendars with filters, capacity %, and highlights
Support HR administration: leave types, blackouts, balance adjustments, and CSV/PDF report export
Display in-app notifications and enforce 30-min idle auto-logout
components.architectureContext: React SPA served by nginx, consuming leave-api via REST/JSON. Sole user-facing entrypoint.
keyConsiderations: Store JWT in memory (not localStorage) and refresh expiry on user activity
Resolve API base URL from window.configs or VITE_API_URL at runtime
Serve on port 8080 via nginx with a /health endpoint
Ensure <2s page loads via code-splitting and memoized calendar rendering
Handle file uploads up to 5MB with client-side mime validation
components.apiContract:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Leave Web
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      responses:
        '200': { description: OK }

components.name: notification-dispatcher
responsibilities: Poll leave-api for pending email notifications every 5 minutes
Dispatch emails via SMTP or log simulation when SMTP_HOST unset
Mark notifications as sent via the leave-api callback
Expose a /health endpoint for liveness before running the job
components.architectureContext: Scheduled Go task authenticating with leave-api via SERVICE_TOKEN; consumes /notifications/pending and POSTs /notifications/{id}/mark-sent. No inbound callers besides health checks.
keyConsiderations: Run once per invocation then exit cleanly for cronjob semantics
Idempotent mark-sent to avoid duplicate email sends on retry
Read LEAVE_API_URL, SERVICE_TOKEN, SMTP_HOST from env
Expose /health on port 9090 before running the job
Handle network failures gracefully with per-notification error isolation
components.apiContract:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Notification Dispatcher
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      responses:
        '200': { description: OK }
